evals.name: question-answering
evals.prompt: Why was autology's Go MCP server removed? What specific problems did it create, and what replaced it?
evals.expected_output: Searches docs/ for 'MCP server' or 'Go' or 'code-less'. Reads codeless-architecture-decision.md. Follows wikilinks (autology-internals, shell-hook-scripts). Cites the 4 documented problems: build/compilation overhead, Go toolchain dependency, complex hook→binary bootstrapping, redundant MCP layers. States what replaced it: Claude native tools (Read/Write/Edit/Grep/Glob) for docs/*.md, shell script hooks. Answer is grounded in the ADR, not general reasoning about Go vs shell.

assertions.description: Grep or search of docs/ is performed before answering — not answered from memory
codeless-architecture-decision.md is read
At least one wikilinked node is followed (1-hop) for additional context
Answer cites the specific ADR doc (e.g., 'per [[codeless-architecture-decision]]')

Answer cites at least 2 of the 4 documented problems: build/compilation overhead, Go toolchain dependency, complex hook→binary bootstrapping, redundant MCP abstraction layers (Claude native tools make MCP unnecessary)

evals.name: overview-operation
evals.expected_output: Reads all docs/*.md, extracts frontmatter and wikilinks. Reports: 8 total nodes, 24 directed links, 1 component, top 5 hub nodes with connection counts, orphan nodes (none), node type breakdown (4 concept, 3 decision, 1 component). Format matches the overview output spec.

assertions.description: All docs/*.md files are globbed and read
Top hub nodes (most wikilink connections) are identified and listed with connection counts
Orphan nodes (no links) are identified — even if result is 'none'
Total node count and link count are reported as numbers
Node type breakdown is included (e.g., how many concept / decision / component nodes)
Type counts are correct: 4 concept, 3 decision, 1 component (8 total)

evals.name: neighborhood-traversal
evals.expected_output: Reads docs/autology-internals.md. Finds ALL nodes that link to or from it (1-hop) — both outgoing wikilinks AND nodes that reference [[autology-internals]] via grep. Finds their connections (2-hop BFS). Reports node title, type, tags, and connections at each hop. Does not stop at 1-hop. codeless-architecture-decision should appear as 1-hop (it links to autology-internals) not 2-hop.

assertions.description: docs/autology-internals.md is read

Nodes that autology-internals links to (outgoing) are identified — autology-system-specification, shell-hook-scripts, autology-user-guide, autology-workflow-reentry-guard, triage-explore-split-decision
docs/ is grepped for [[autology-internals]] to find nodes linking TO the target — codeless-architecture-decision is a 1-hop neighbor via incoming link
2-hop neighbors (neighbors of neighbors not yet visited) are also found and reported
Each node in the output includes its type and tags

evals.name: path-query
evals.expected_output: Reads docs/autology-philosophy.md and docs/shell-hook-scripts.md. Finds no direct wikilink between them. Identifies the 2-hop path: autology-philosophy → codeless-architecture-decision → shell-hook-scripts (autology-philosophy links to [[codeless-architecture-decision]], which links to [[shell-hook-scripts]]). Reports path as: autology-philosophy → codeless-architecture-decision → shell-hook-scripts with hop labels.

assertions.description: Both docs/autology-philosophy.md and docs/shell-hook-scripts.md are read
codeless-architecture-decision is identified as the intermediate node connecting the two endpoints
Output shows the 2-hop path in A → B → C format with hop labels
Path length is reported as 2 hops — no shorter path exists and no spurious extra hops are added

evals.name: reentry-guard-conditions
evals.prompt: What specific conditions trigger the autology-workflow reentry guard? List the exact criteria that count as 'autology-workflow just completed', and explain why the check is event-based rather than state-based.
evals.expected_output: Searches docs/ for 'reentry guard'. Reads autology-workflow-reentry-guard.md. Reports BOTH specific conditions: (1) triage-knowledge, sync-knowledge, or capture-knowledge just ran; (2) a docs/*.md file was just written or edited. Explains the event-vs-state distinction: state-based is fragile (state can become stale or be missed); event-based looks backward at the source of the completed action with no state to track. Cites [[autology-workflow-reentry-guard]].

assertions.description: docs/ is searched before answering — not answered from general knowledge about recursion prevention
autology-workflow-reentry-guard.md is read
Answer correctly states the skill-based condition: triage-knowledge, sync-knowledge, or capture-knowledge just ran
Answer correctly states the file-based condition: a docs/*.md file was just written or edited (counterintuitive — not covered by general recursion reasoning)
Answer explains WHY event-based was chosen over state-based: state can become stale or be missed; event-based looks backward at what completed with no state to track
